title: Variable-Data Event Promo Catalog + Price-Tag Merge (Overset-Safe Auto-Fit Template)
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief: the client runs variable-data merges into printing projects, and their core pain is that "InDesign can't automatically reduce font sizes if they are too long for the frame" — text runs overset / gets clipped when long records are merged, and they want the export to come out clean ("automatically shrink overset text when exporting"). The brief is literally phrased as a request to BUILD an InDesign plugin (a from-scratch software-development job), which is out of scope for the Adobe connector and for any design deliverable. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe data-merge benchmark while staying faithful to the brief's actual intent: instead of writing a plugin, we treat the overset problem the way the connector legitimately can — the client authors the .indd merge template with InDesign's native auto-fit / "shrink text to fit frame" (auto-size text frames) already enabled on the variable-data frames, and the agent runs the variable-data merge + export through that overset-safe template so long records render without clipping. I invented a concrete event/promotion product (a party-supply / event-promo product catalog with one card per SKU, plus matching price tags) because the brief's vertical is "Party, Events & Promotion" and gives no specific product; the field names, CSV columns, and bleed/spec numbers are reasonable trade-print conventions, not taken from the brief. The auto-fit behavior is the client's stated requirement, not an invented one — it is baked into the authored template inputs ([T] steps), which is the honest connector path since document_merge_data_layout/vector require a user-authored .indd/.ai carrying real merge fields. No plugin is written; the deliverable is the merged, overset-safe print PDFs the plugin was meant to produce.
one_line_ask: Run our variable-data merge into the auto-fit (shrink-to-fit) InDesign catalog template and the Illustrator price-tag template so long product names no longer overset, prepping the product hero art and licensed background first, and export print-ready PDFs.
full_brief: We print a per-event promotional product catalog and matching shelf price tags, and our records come from a spreadsheet (one row per SKU: product name, blurb, price, event, SKU). Our recurring headache is that when a product name or blurb is long, the merged text overflows the frame and gets clipped on export — InDesign won't shrink it to fit on its own. We have already rebuilt our InDesign catalog template (catalog_template.indd) and our Illustrator price-tag template (pricetag_template.ai) with auto-size / "shrink text to fit frame" enabled on every variable-data frame and real merge fields (<<ProductName>>, <<Blurb>>, <<Price>>, <<SKU>>, <<EventName>>) wired up, so the layout itself is now overset-safe. We need you to: (1) stand up an asset workspace; (2) take in our authored templates, our catalog data (catalog_data.csv, ~24 SKUs), our product hero photo (hero_product.jpg) and our logo (logo_master.png); (3) prep the art that flows into the merge — isolate the product from its messy shot, clean the tone, build a clean bleed-safe background by cropping a licensed stock texture to the A4-plus-bleed frame, and vectorize the logo so it stays crisp at any print size; (4) run the data merge into BOTH the catalog .indd (one catalog card per row) and the price-tag .ai (Illustrator Variables, one tag per row); (5) export both as print-ready PDFs (catalog at A4 with bleed, tags at tag size); and (6) hand back the finished print-ready files. The whole point: long records must render legibly via the template's auto-fit, never clipped.

inputs.name: catalog_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: USER-AUTHORED Adobe InDesign document (.indd / .idml). A single A4 portrait catalog card master (210x297mm + 3mm bleed) with real InDesign Data Merge fields placed as <<ProductName>>, <<Blurb>>, <<Price>>, <<SKU>>, <<EventName>>, plus an image frame placeholder for the prepped product art and one for the background. CRITICAL per the brief: every text frame holding a variable field has 'Auto-Size: Height Only / Shrink text to fit' (auto-fit) enabled so long merged strings shrink instead of going overset. This file is authored in desktop InDesign and supplied by the client; it cannot be generated by the connector and is the required input for the [T] document_merge_data_layout step.
inputs.realism_notes: Must be a genuine .indd/.idml with bindable data-merge fields and auto-size enabled on the variable frames — a PDF-converted .indd with literal text will NOT bind. Field names must exactly match the CSV column headers.

inputs.name: pricetag_template.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: USER-AUTHORED Adobe Illustrator document (.ai) sized as a 70x40mm shelf price tag with Illustrator Variables bound (Object > Variables) to dynamic text objects for ProductName, Price, SKU and a linked-image variable for the logo. Long-name objects use area-type with 'Auto Size' so they shrink to fit. Supplied by the client; required input for the [T] document_merge_data_vector step.
inputs.realism_notes: Must carry real Illustrator Variables (not just static text); the variable names must match the CSV columns. Connector cannot author this — it is a client deliverable.

inputs.name: catalog_data.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic ~24-row CSV for an event/party-supply promo catalog with header row: ProductName,Blurb,Price,SKU,EventName,@ImagePath. Include a deliberate mix of SHORT and DELIBERATELY LONG product names and blurbs (e.g. one ProductName ~6 chars like 'Confetti Cannon' and one ~58 chars like 'Deluxe Personalised Helium-Filled Foil Number Balloon Bundle (Gold)') so the auto-fit/overset behavior is actually exercised. Prices like '$12.99', SKUs like 'EVT-0142', EventName values like 'Summer Gala 2026'. The @ImagePath column holds the data-merge image path token.
inputs.realism_notes: Column headers must EXACTLY match the .indd merge fields and .ai variables. The long/short length spread is the whole point — it's what triggers the shrink-to-fit the brief is about.

inputs.name: hero_product.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal product shot of a single colourful party/event product — a glossy gold foil number balloon bundle with curling ribbon — sitting on a cluttered, slightly messy studio tabletop with visible seams, a wrinkled paper backdrop, and uneven natural window light. Real specular highlights on the foil, real soft shadows, a few stray ribbon strands, candid documentary feel, NOT a clean e-commerce cutout, NOT CGI-glossy. 4000x3000px.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately shot on a messy background with imperfect tone so the remove-background + auto-tone steps have real work to do. Photoreal, real reflections and shadows, not a pre-cut PNG.

inputs.name: logo_master.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean flat 2-colour event-brand wordmark logo reading 'PARTYWORKS' in a bold geometric sans with a small confetti-burst mark, solid fills, no gradients, on a transparent background. Crisp edges suitable for vectorization. 2048x1024px PNG.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat solid-fill raster logo (text + simple mark) so image_vectorize can produce a clean SVG that stays sharp at print sizes. Transparent background.

inputs.name: bg_texture_ref
inputs.gen_prompt: A soft festive bokeh / subtle confetti-texture studio background, muted pastel gradient, slightly out of focus, photographic. Used only as a search/visual reference for licensing a comparable Adobe Stock texture. 2000x2000px.
inputs.realism_notes: Reference image to ground the asset_search/stock-license step; the actual background used in the deliverable comes from the licensed full-res stock asset, not this ref.

outputs.name: catalog_merged_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready multipage PDF, one A4 portrait catalog card per CSV row (~24 pages), 210x297mm + 3mm bleed, exported from the merged .indd via document_render_layout. Long product names/blurbs rendered shrunk-to-fit (no overset clipping). Prepped product art and bleed-safe background placed.

outputs.name: pricetags_merged.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready PDF of merged shelf price tags, 70x40mm each, one per CSV row, exported from the merged .ai via document_render_vector. Vectorized logo placed; long names auto-sized.

outputs.name: logo_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the brand wordmark produced by image_vectorize from logo_master.png, used inside the merge so the mark stays crisp at any print size.

outputs.name: product_art_prepped.png
outputs.spec: Background-removed, auto-toned, cropped-to-cell product hero PNG (transparent) used as the merge image for the hero SKU.

connector_workflow.note: Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /AO-62/templates, /art, /merged) to hold templates, prepped art and outputs.
Initialize upload sessions into the workspace for the client-authored templates (catalog_template.indd, pricetag_template.ai), catalog_data.csv, hero_product.jpg and logo_master.png.
Finalize the uploads so the .indd, .ai, .csv, hero photo and logo are real CC assets with URNs available to later steps.
Using bg_texture_ref as the visual target, search Adobe Stock, LICENSE (before editing) and download the full-res festive bokeh background texture for the catalog backdrop.
Geometrically crop (no generation) the licensed full-res texture to the A4-plus-bleed aspect box (216x303mm ratio), reframing the existing pixels to cover trim+bleed with no edge gaps.
Crop/resize the reframed background to the exact 216x303mm bleed pixel box at print resolution for placement in the catalog frame.
Isolate the foil-balloon product from its messy studio tabletop → transparent PNG.
Auto-tone the isolated product to fix the uneven window-light exposure/contrast.
Lift vibrance/saturation so the gold foil reads punchy on the printed card.
Crop-and-resize the prepped product to the catalog image-cell aspect/size → product_art_prepped.png, the merge image for the hero SKU.
Vectorize logo_master.png → clean logo_vector.svg so the brand mark stays crisp at any print size inside both templates.

[T] InDesign Data Merge: bind catalog_data.csv into the client-authored catalog_template.indd (real <<fields>>, auto-fit frames), pulling the prepped product art, bleed background and vector logo → one catalog card per row. Auto-size frames shrink long records (the brief's overset fix).
Export the merged .indd to print-ready multipage PDF (A4 + 3mm bleed) → catalog_merged_print.pdf.
[T] Illustrator Variables: bind catalog_data.csv into the client-authored pricetag_template.ai (variables + auto-size area type), placing the vector logo → one 70x40mm tag per row.
Export the merged .ai to print-ready PDF → pricetags_merged.pdf.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-3 stand up the workspace and finalize the client-supplied assets into uploaded_assets (the only initial load). Background branch: licensed_bg (4, from stock) → bg_bleed (5, geometric crop to the A4+bleed aspect box — reframes existing licensed pixels, invents nothing) → bg_final (6, resize to exact 216x303mm print box). Product branch: hero_cutout (7, from uploaded hero) → hero_toned (8) → hero_pop (9) → product_art_prepped (10). Logo branch: logo_vector (11, vectorize uploaded logo). Each of these consumes only a prior step's named output. The two [T] merges are the convergence point: document_merge_data_layout (12) consumes uploaded_assets (the authored .indd + CSV) PLUS product_art_prepped + bg_final + logo_vector; its output catalog_merged_indd feeds render_layout (13). document_merge_data_vector (14) consumes the authored .ai + logo_vector; its output feeds render_vector (15). Nothing after step 3 reads a raw input that wasn't routed through a prior step's output.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 15 steps, 14 distinct Adobe tools across 3 groups (asset/stock, image/raster, document/layout-vector). It exercises three independent prep branches (stock→reframe-crop→crop, cutout→tone→vibrance→crop, vectorize) that converge into a two-format variable-data merge, then export. Difficulty comes from (a) honoring the [T] reality — the merges only work because the client authored real .indd/.ai templates with bindable fields/variables AND the auto-fit/shrink-to-fit that solves the brief's overset complaint, so the agent must recognize it cannot 'fix overset' itself and must route field-matched CSV/art into the authored template; (b) exact field-name/column alignment between CSV, .indd fields and .ai variables; (c) the generative step being legitimately only image_crop_and_resize (reframe-crop), with no gen-fill/object-removal/compositing anywhere; (d) licensing stock BEFORE editing. The deliverable genuinely emerges from the chain rather than any single call.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability anywhere. Step 5 is image_crop_to_bounds — a purely geometric crop that reframes existing licensed-stock pixels to the A4+bleed aspect box, inventing no pixels; step 6 image_crop_and_resize resizes that crop to the exact 216x303mm print box (never larger than the source in any grown dimension). No image_crop_and_resize/reframe-crop, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no gen-fill, no AI object-removal-with-reconstruction, no bg-replace-by-prompt, no image→PDF misuse, no per-artboard named export. image_remove_background is a mask/cutout (allowed, non-generative). document_merge_data_layout/_vector remain [T] on genuine user-authored .indd/.ai templates carrying real bindable merge fields/variables; document_render_layout/_vector only export the real merged docs. Stock licensed at step 4 BEFORE any edit. All prose (full_brief, outputs[].spec, chaining_note, difficulty_rationale) now describes the gen-free crop path — no residual 'outpaint'/'generative expand' wording remains.
reverify.notes: Only cosmetic scrubs applied: removed the stale 'reframe-crop'/'image_crop_and_resize' references from chaining_note and difficulty_rationale so the prose matches the already-corrected step 5 (image_crop_to_bounds). Low-value extras trimmed (Firefly review board + redundant re-bundling zip + standalone font-recommendation .txt removed); 15 total calls, 14 distinct Adobe tools. Task remains real, coherent, and fully connector-doable.
